output:
**Day 8.**

According to the security engineer's description:

- Access tokens expire every 15 minutes (silently refreshed using the refresh token)
- Refresh tokens expire every 7 days
- After the refresh token expires, the user **must** re-authenticate

The user logs in on Day 1. Their refresh token expires after 7 days. On **Day 8**, when their access token expires and the system attempts to use the refresh token, it will have expired — forcing the user to re-enter their credentials.

(The PM's description of "30 days, no re-auth" directly contradicts the security engineer's implementation. This is a common real-world disconnect between product intent and actual security controls.)
